Technical Appendix
Home Demo Hub Presentation Simulator System Story System Scale Agent Hierarchy Agent Registry Operating Model Validator Map Safety Boundaries Technical Appendix Objections Review Full Audit Dashboard
Home → Demo Hub → Technical Appendix

Architecture summary

Static, version-controlled, and intentionally read-only.

The technical appendix explains how the dashboard is assembled, how the validators work, what they prove, and what still needs a separate runtime activation phase.

Known limitations

Validators prove content and boundary markers, not live runtime behavior.

Architecture summary

Static HTML/CSS/JS in the Netlify publish directory, with markdown source docs in the repo and validators in Python.

Validator strategy

Milestone validators, package validators, a dynamic latest-status check, and a master wall keep the release story consistent.

Flat E2E strategy

Independent validators avoid nested chains and make failures easier to isolate.

Dynamic latest-status strategy

The live dashboard must still report MVP-50 as the latest verified milestone.

Safety-denial marker strategy

The system uses explicit markers such as NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION to keep runtime intent unambiguous.

Future runtime activation gates

Security review, approval gates, monitoring, rollback planning, and change-management sign-off remain required before any runtime phase.

Glossary

Translate the demo terms into plain English

These definitions keep the appendix readable for non-technical reviewers.

Registered agent

A registered command-center operating unit listed in the static org-chart registry.

Live runtime agent

A live autonomous process. The count is 0 in this build.

Runtime activation

Turning on live execution. It has not started.

Static simulator

A browser-only in-memory demo that does not call backends or execute actions.

Backend/Supabase readiness

The architecture is prepared, but live writes and public mutation remain disabled.

Validator

A script that checks files, markers, and safety conditions.

Safety gate

A hard stop that prevents unsafe movement into runtime or mutation flows.

MVP-50

The current verified demo milestone, summarized here as MVP-50 Production Verified.

This appendix is mirrored in `technical_reviewer_appendix.md`.
